pending.body: finding_summary: `materialize_beads()` cannot reliably distinguish a workstream epic from a task bead when both are non-root epics. `identity_label_matches()` treats any legacy bead that is missing exporter identity labels as a match, and `materialized_role_matches()` collapses `WorkstreamEpic` and `Task` into the same bucket. On a rerun against an older or label-drifted export, a proposal with `bead_type: "epic"` and the same title as its workstream can therefore reuse the workstream epic as the task bead; the later parent-child link then targets the same bead and corrupts the hierarchy. This comes from the reuse path in `find_existing_materialized_bead()` relying on those predicates. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1936-1950`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2034-2070`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2117-2159`. remediation_hint: make role matching distinguish workstream epics from task beads even for legacy unlabeled beads, or fail closed instead of allowing unlabeled non-root epics to satisfy task reuse.

finding_summary: explicit bead-id reuse accepts any canonical/short-id hit before title matching, without validating that the existing bead still matches the proposal’s type or role. In `find_explicit_materialized_bead()`, any `br show` result for the explicit id is accepted if `explicit_materialized_bead_matches()` returns true; that helper only checks the id and milestone/identity labels, not whether the bead still looks like the proposal being exported. A stale or mistyped `bead_id` in the bundle can therefore hijack an unrelated existing bead and attach the current workstream dependency/comments to it. affected_files: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2259-2321`. remediation_hint: after resolving an explicit bead by id, still require at least bead-type/role compatibility (and ideally some title or provenance sanity check), while preserving the ability to tolerate title drift for genuinely reused authored beads.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 11 -> Plan and Implement
